methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $575M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
VulcanForms is a late-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by John Hart that has raised $575M+. The valuation analysis draws from a sector universe of 9 peers—1 public and 8 private—including comparable companies such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised of $575M as a proxy base value and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for late-stage progression, this method yields an implied valuation of $690M. The peer-median method could not be applied due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables; while 11 same-sector peers exist in robotics and manufacturing, none met the minimum threshold of three datapoints when filtered by same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier criteria. A broader sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of robotics subsectors.

The triangulated valuation range is $586M (low) to $793M (high), with a median of $690M. This single-method result carries material uncertainty given the reliance on stage-progression mechanics rather than market-based peer multiples. Key uncertainties include the absence of adequate late-stage robotics comparables and the degree to which $575M in historical fundraising accurately reflects current enterprise value. Additional disclosure of revenue, profitability, and customer concentration would substantially improve conviction in this estimate.
